documentation_gaps.title: Add troubleshooting guide with common errors and solutions
documentation_gaps.description: Create docs/troubleshooting.md covering common failure modes: stale locks, orphaned cards, adapter timeouts, policy check failures, VCS conflicts, and rate limiting. Include diagnostic commands and remediation steps.
documentation_gaps.rationale: Users frequently encounter card failures but have no systematic guide to diagnose root causes. Common errors like 'dispatcher lock held' or 'policy check failed' have known solutions that should be documented.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: README has basic CLI usage but no error diagnostics
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Add docs/troubleshooting.md with sections for: lock debugging (bop doctor, manual lock removal), failed card recovery (bop retry, log inspection), adapter debugging (exit code meanings, timeout tuning), policy violations (understanding diff output, fixing runtime path issues), VCS engine troubleshooting (jj vs git workflow differences)

documentation_gaps.title: Document Rust API with module-level doc comments
documentation_gaps.description: Add module-level documentation (//! comments) to all bop-cli and bop-core modules explaining purpose, key types, and usage patterns. Currently only 128 doc comments across 20+ files in bop-cli.
documentation_gaps.rationale: Contributors need to understand module responsibilities and public API contracts. Module-level docs provide entry points for understanding the codebase architecture.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: bop-core has 214 doc comments (better coverage), bop-cli has only 128 doc comments for many more files
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Add //! module docs to: dispatcher.rs (orchestration logic), cards.rs (card lifecycle), serve.rs (HTTP API), merge_gate.rs (merge workflow), reaper.rs (orphan handling), workspace.rs (VCS integration), policy.rs (anti-slop gates). Include examples showing typical usage patterns.

documentation_gaps.title: Create adapter development guide
documentation_gaps.description: Add docs/adapter-development.md explaining how to write new adapters, exit code semantics (0=success, 75=rate-limit, other=fail), error handling patterns, timeout handling, MCP config integration, and memory-out.json format.
documentation_gaps.rationale: CONTRIBUTING.md says 'Copy adapters/mock.nu as starting point' but provides no context on error handling, rate limiting detection, or MCP integration. Developers need comprehensive guidance.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: CONTRIBUTING.md has 5-line adapter section, adapters have minimal inline comments
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Document: calling convention (5 args), exit code contract, rate-limit detection patterns (stderr parsing for '429', 'rate limit', 'too many requests'), timeout wrapper usage (perl alarm pattern), MCP config merging (.cards/mcp.json + card-level), memory output format, provider rotation triggers, CLAUDECODE env handling

documentation_gaps.title: Add architecture diagrams and data flow documentation
documentation_gaps.description: Create docs/architecture.md with diagrams showing: card state machine transitions, dispatcher orchestration flow, merge-gate pipeline, VCS engine integration points, adapter invocation lifecycle, OpenLineage provenance recording.
documentation_gaps.rationale: README has a simple text diagram but lacks detailed component interaction diagrams. New contributors need visual representations of the state machine, dispatcher loop, and merge workflow.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: README has basic ASCII diagram, format-storage-contract.md has state machine paths, no flow diagrams
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Add Mermaid diagrams showing: 1) Card lifecycle (pending→running→done→merged with failure paths), 2) Dispatcher poll loop with reaper integration, 3) Merge-gate stages (policy→test→PR creation), 4) VCS workspace preparation flow for git vs jj, 5) Adapter invocation with timeout/rate-limit handling, 6) OpenLineage event emission points

documentation_gaps.title: Document configuration system and environment variables
documentation_gaps.description: Create docs/configuration.md documenting ~/.bop/config.json schema, .cards/config.json overrides, environment variables (BOP_SERVE_TOKEN, BOP_MEMORY_OUT, CLAUDECODE), and card-level meta.json fields with examples.
documentation_gaps.rationale: Config schema exists in bop-core/src/config.rs but is not documented for users. Multiple config layers (.bop/config.json, .cards/config.json, meta.json) are undocumented. Environment variables are scattered across code.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: config.rs has inline comments but no user-facing docs
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Document: 1) Config hierarchy (global → project → card), 2) Schema for config.json (default_provider_chain, max_concurrent, cooldown_seconds, log_retention_days, default_template), 3) Environment variables table with purposes, 4) meta.json schema with all 40+ fields explained, 5) Config examples (provider chains, timeouts, stage pipelines, team routing)

documentation_gaps.title: Add 'Getting Started' tutorial with walkthrough
documentation_gaps.description: Create docs/getting-started.md with step-by-step tutorial: install bop, init workspace, create first card, run dispatcher, inspect logs, handle failures, merge successful card. Include expected output at each step.
documentation_gaps.rationale: README Quick Start shows commands but lacks narrative walkthrough showing what success looks like. New users need hand-holding through first successful card execution.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: README has Quick Start commands but no tutorial format
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Tutorial covering: 1) Install from source or DMG, 2) bop init and directory structure explanation, 3) bop new implement hello-world, 4) Edit spec.md with simple task, 5) bop dispatcher --once --adapter adapters/mock.nu, 6) Watch state transitions (pending→running→done), 7) bop inspect hello-world, 8) bop logs hello-world, 9) bop merge-gate --once, 10) Understanding success (card in merged/, git commit created)

documentation_gaps.title: Document VCS engine differences (git vs jj)
documentation_gaps.description: Create docs/vcs-engines.md explaining git+GitGt vs jj workflow differences, workspace/worktree semantics, branch naming conventions, merge strategies, and when to use each engine.
documentation_gaps.rationale: System memory states 'VCS: jj (Jujutsu)' but dispatcher defaults to git. GitGt vs jj code paths exist but behavioral differences are undocumented. Users don't know which to choose or how they differ.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: No VCS engine comparison docs, workspace.rs has implementation but no user guidance
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Document: 1) GitGt engine: git worktree-based isolation, job/<id> branches, squash-merge to main, 2) jj engine: jj workspace per card, change_ref tracking, workspace lifecycle, 3) Comparison table (isolation model, undo capabilities, branch cleanup), 4) Recommendation: jj for multi-agent teams, git for simpler workflows, 5) Migration path between engines, 6) Troubleshooting VCS-specific issues

documentation_gaps.title: Add CHANGELOG.md tracking version history
documentation_gaps.description: Create CHANGELOG.md following Keep a Changelog format, documenting changes since initial release. Backfill major milestones: Nushell migration (2026-03-03), bop serve addition (spec 015), VCS engine abstraction.
documentation_gaps.rationale: No CHANGELOG exists. Users and contributors cannot track what changed between versions or understand breaking changes. Standard practice for open source projects.
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Create CHANGELOG.md with: [Unreleased] section, version entries with [Added], [Changed], [Deprecated], [Removed], [Fixed], [Security] sections. Backfill: v0.2.0 (Nushell migration, all 24 scripts ported), v0.3.0 (bop serve HTTP API, GitGt/jj abstraction), v0.1.0 (initial release with ZSH scripts). Link to GitHub releases.

documentation_gaps.title: Add SECURITY.md with vulnerability reporting process
documentation_gaps.description: Create SECURITY.md documenting security policy, supported versions, vulnerability reporting process, and responsible disclosure guidelines. Currently only .auto-claude-security.json exists.
documentation_gaps.rationale: GitHub displays SECURITY.md in Security tab. Essential for open source projects to document how security issues should be reported. Current .auto-claude-security.json is not user-facing.
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Document: 1) Supported versions (e.g., 'Latest release + previous minor version'), 2) Reporting process (email address or GitHub Security Advisories), 3) Response timeline expectations, 4) Disclosure policy (coordinated disclosure window), 5) Security considerations (sandbox mode, BOP_SERVE_TOKEN, .cards/ permission model)

documentation_gaps.title: Document Nushell script organization and conventions
documentation_gaps.description: Create docs/scripting.md explaining script organization (adapters/ vs scripts/ vs vibekanban/), calling conventions, self-test pattern (--test flag), error handling idioms, and Nushell-specific patterns used in bop.
documentation_gaps.rationale: 16 scripts in scripts/, 9 adapters, 2 vibekanban scripts - all using Nushell with minimal documentation. Contributors need guidance on conventions: self-test pattern, exit code handling, $env.LAST_EXIT_CODE usage, hide-env patterns.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: CONTRIBUTING says 'All shell scripts: #!/usr/bin/env nu' but no conventions documented
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Document: 1) Script directory purposes (adapters=AI providers, scripts=utilities, vibekanban=UI), 2) Standard patterns: --test self-tests with std/assert, exit code conventions, path resolution (absolute vs relative), 3) Common idioms: $env.LAST_EXIT_CODE for exit codes, hide-env for isolation, do { ^cmd } | complete for error capture, 4) Integration points with Rust binary, 5) Testing scripts: nu script.nu --test

documentation_gaps.title: Add production deployment guide
documentation_gaps.description: Create docs/deployment.md covering launchd/systemd setup, log rotation, monitoring recommendations, scaling guidance, backup strategies, and production configuration examples.
documentation_gaps.rationale: README mentions 'launchd is underrated infrastructure' and lessons.md discusses KeepAlive, but no step-by-step deployment guide exists. Production users need operational guidance.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: cicd-integration.md covers GitHub Actions but not production deployment
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Document: 1) launchd setup (plist example with KeepAlive, WatchPaths, throttling), 2) systemd setup (.service + .path units), 3) Log management (stdout/stderr redirection, rotation with logrotate), 4) Monitoring: dispatcher health checks, card queue metrics, failure rate alerts, 5) Scaling: max_workers tuning, disk space monitoring, 6) Backup: .cards/ directory strategy, database-free advantage, 7) Troubleshooting production issues

documentation_gaps.title: Document card template system and variables
documentation_gaps.description: Expand template documentation in CONTRIBUTING.md or create docs/templates.md explaining template structure, variable substitution ({{spec}}, {{plan}}, {{stage}}, etc.), stage_chain usage, and how to create custom templates.
documentation_gaps.rationale: 9 templates exist (.cards/templates/) but only implement.bop/spec.md has minimal example. Template variable system (15+ variables in PromptContext) is undocumented. Users can't create custom templates without reading source.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: CONTRIBUTING has 7-line template section, templates have minimal inline docs
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Document: 1) Template directory structure (meta.json, spec.md, prompt.md, logs/, output/), 2) meta.json schema (stage, stage_chain, provider_chain, acceptance_criteria, stage_models, stage_providers), 3) Variable substitution: {{spec}}, {{plan}}, {{stage}}, {{acceptance_criteria}}, {{provider}}, {{agent}}, {{memory}}, {{system_context}}, {{stage_instructions}}, {{worktree_branch}}, {{prior_stage_output}}, {{depends_output}}, 4) Creating custom templates, 5) Example: multi-stage pipeline template

documentation_gaps.title: Add inline comments for complex algorithms in dispatcher
documentation_gaps.description: Add explanatory comments to complex logic in dispatcher.rs: dependency resolution loop, lease heartbeat calculation, reaper orphan detection, provider rotation on rate-limit, and state transition validation.
documentation_gaps.rationale: dispatcher.rs has complex concurrent logic (reaper, dependency checking, slot management) with minimal inline comments. Contributors need guidance to understand non-obvious invariants and edge cases.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: Minimal inline comments, no algorithm explanations
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Add comments explaining: 1) Dependency resolution algorithm (how depends_on is checked across states), 2) Lease heartbeat timing (why 3x reap_ms threshold), 3) Orphan detection criteria (dead PID vs stale lease), 4) Provider rotation logic (exit 75 → next provider), 5) Slot counting invariants (why running_count uses .bop extension filter), 6) State transition atomicity (rename guarantees), 7) OpenLineage event timing (when events are emitted)

documentation_gaps.title: Create FAQ section in docs
documentation_gaps.description: Add docs/faq.md answering common questions: Why filesystem state machine? Why no database? How to handle rate limiting? What's the difference between .bop and .jobcard? How to debug stuck cards? How to add a new adapter? When to use jj vs git?
documentation_gaps.rationale: Recurring questions about design decisions (filesystem vs database, APFS COW, no daemon) and operational issues (stuck cards, rate limits) should be documented centrally.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: lessons.md explains philosophy but not in Q&A format, no FAQ
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: FAQ covering: 1) Why filesystem state machine? (atomic mv, no daemon, Finder native), 2) Why APFS COW? (template cloning efficiency, dedupe), 3) .bop vs .jobcard? (same format, different convention), 4) Handling rate limits? (exit 75, provider rotation), 5) Stuck cards? (bop kill, manual state transition), 6) Adding adapters? (link to adapter-development.md), 7) VCS engine choice? (link to vcs-engines.md), 8) Performance tuning? (max_workers, poll_ms, reap_ms)

documentation_gaps.title: Document testing strategy and harness usage
documentation_gaps.description: Create docs/testing.md explaining test organization (dispatcher_harness, job_control_harness, merge_gate_harness), how to add tests, running specific test suites, and testing Nushell scripts (--test flag pattern).
documentation_gaps.rationale: 3 test harnesses exist (19KB, 18KB, 5KB of test code) but no documentation on how to use them or add new tests. CONTRIBUTING says 'cargo test' but doesn't explain test architecture.
documentation_gaps.currentDocumentation: CONTRIBUTING has 'cargo test' command, no test architecture docs
documentation_gaps.proposedContent: Document: 1) Test harness purposes (dispatcher_harness=end-to-end card workflow, job_control_harness=CLI commands, merge_gate_harness=merge flow), 2) Running tests: cargo test, cargo test -p bop-cli, cargo test dispatcher, 3) Writing new tests: harness patterns, tempdir setup, state assertions, 4) Testing scripts: nu script.nu --test, nu scripts/test-real-adapters.nu, 5) Integration tests vs unit tests, 6) CI expectations (make check = test + clippy + fmt)
